commands.review_summary:
This PR sorts Feishu mention placeholders by descending key length before normalization and adds colocated tests for prefix-sharing keys plus adjacent text.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +23. Total +26 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main replaces mention keys in event order with a global regex, so a message containing @_user_1 and @_user_10 can mis-normalize the longer key before its own replacement runs; I did not execute tests because this cleanup review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because no blocking findings were found and the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review or landing.

target.title: fix(feishu): replace longer mention keys first
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Feishu inbound mention normalization when mention keys share prefixes, such as `@_user_1`, `@_user_10`, and `@_user_11`.

Feishu sends the stable mention identity in `message.mentions`; the text body only contains per-message placeholder keys. The previous replacement loop processed keys in event order, so replacing `@_user_1` first could rewrite the prefix inside `@_user_10` and prevent the longer key from ever resolving.

This patch keeps the existing source-of-truth behavior from `mentions`, but replaces longer keys first. That fixes the prefix collision without adding a delimiter rule, so adjacent text like `@_user_1thanks` still normalizes the listed mention and preserves the suffix.

Supersedes the approach in #96284.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot-content.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.stripBotMention.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot-content.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.stripBotMention.test.ts"`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu mention placeholders with shared prefixes are normalized to the wrong user when a shorter key is replaced before a longer key.

Real environment tested: local Vitest through the repo `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot-content.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.stripBotMention.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  13 passed (13)
```

Observed result after fix: `@_user_10` and `@_user_11` resolve to their own users even when `@_user_1` is also present, and `@_user_1thanks` still normalizes the listed mention while preserving `thanks`.

What was not tested: live Feishu group round-trip; the patch covers the pure normalization function used by `parseFeishuMessageEvent`.
